State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • •     Administration • •     Executive/Legal • •     Enforcement
(916) 322-5562                    322-5660                 322-5901                 322-6441

June 29, 1984


W. J. Anthony, Director
Department of General Services
State & Consumer Services Agency
915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                         Our No. A-84-159

Dear Mr. Anthony:

     You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted
air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable
income for those officials if the discount opportunity is
negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial
carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and
officials traveling on state business.  We understand that you
have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on the
issue of whether or not this is permissible under California
Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19.  Because the Attorney
General's opinion could determine that such a program is
prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot.
Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney
General has acted.  If the Attorney General determines that such
a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you
advice.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    Robert E. Leidigh
                                    Counsel
                                    Legal Division

REL:plh

State of California                                    State and Consumer Services Agency


Memorandum


                                                      MAY 31 8 33 AM '84

Date  :  May 29, 1984                                File No. :

To    :  Dan Stanford, Chairperson               Subject :
         Fair Political Practices Commission
         1100 K Street -- D-23




From  :  Department of General Services


On May 3, 1984, I asked for your advice on whether an officer and employee
of the State, taking advantage of the discounted rates for personal trips,
under the contracts being consummated with PSA and Delta Airlines, will
have to report the dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules.

We have now determined that the extension of the discounted rates to
management personnel for personal trips will not be included in the con-
tract for this year.  However, we would appreciate your advice on the
question asked in our memorandum, inasmuch as we will have that problem
before us as we enter into contract negotiations next year.

If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your
staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua-
tion (323-3066), or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal Services
(322-5944).


         [signature]

W. J. ANTHONY, Director
Department of General Services

WJA:EJR:dh

cc:  Jack Smith, Chief
     Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation

.e morandum

State and Consumer Services Agenc

FPFC

MAY 7 9 07 AM '84

Date : May 3, 1984                                                File No. :

To : Dan Stanford, Chairman                                        Subject :
     Fair Political Practices Commission
     1100 K Street
     D-23

From : Department of General Services

     Our Department is consummating a contract with PSA and Delta Airlines for
discounted air travel for officers and employees of the State. Under the
contract, the airlines will extend the discounted rates to management per-
sonnel of the State when they travel on personal matters. We would
appreciate your early advice on whether an officer or an employee of the
State, who takes advantage of the discounted rate, will have to report the
dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules.

     If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your
staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua-
tion (323-3066), or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal Services
(322-5944).


W. J. ANTHONY, Director
Department of General Services

WJA:EJR:dh

cc: Jack Smith, Chief
     Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901           322-6441

June 29, 1984


W. J. Anthony, Director
Department of General Services
State & Consumer Services Agency
915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-84-159

Dear Mr. Anthony:

You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted
air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable
income for those officials if the discount opportunity is
negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial
carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and
officials traveling on state business. We understand that you
have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on
the issue of whether or not this is permissible under California
Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19. Because the Attorney
General's Opinion could determine that such a program is
prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot.
Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney
General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such
a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you
advice.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901          322-6441


June 29, 1984


W. J. Anthony, Director
Department of General Services
State & Consumer Services Agency
915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590
Sacramento, CA 95814

                                        Re: Your Request for Advice
                                            Our No. A-84-159

Dear Mr. Anthony:

     You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted
air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable
income for those officials if the discount opportunity is
negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial
carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and
officials traveling on state business. We understand that you
have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on the
issue of whether or not this is permissible under California
Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19. Because the Attorney
General's Opinion could determine that such a program is
prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot.
Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney
General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such
a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you
advice.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert B. Leidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

State of California                                    State and Consumer Services Agency


Memorandum


Date  :   DEC 10 1984                                  Dec 12  10 AM '04
                                                            File No. :

To    :   Richard Martland                            Subject :
          Assistant Attorney General
          Department of Justice
          1515 K Street, Suite 511
          Sacramento, CA  95814    D-8


From  :   Department of General Services


          On May 29, 1984 I transmitted to you a request to provide advice on an issue
          related to air transportation for offices and employees of the State at
          discounted rates. As a result of subsequent discussions with the Department
          of Personnel Administration I have now determined that this advice is no
          longer required. I appreciate the work you have done on this project and
          thank you for your cooperation.

          If you need further information or assistance on this issue, you may wish to
          have your staff contact Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Management Technology
          and Planning at 323-3066, or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal
          Services at 322-5944.

          Original signed by
          Director
          Department of General Services

          W. J. ANTHONY, Director
          Department of General Services

          WJA:JS:bw

          cc:  Bob Leidigh, FPPC

Our Department is consummating a contract with PSA and Delta Airlines for
discounted air travel for officers and employees of the State. Under the
contract, the airlines will extend the discounted rates to management per-
sonnel of the State when they travel on personal matters. We would
appreciate your early advice on whether an officer or an employee of the
State, who takes advantage of the discounted rate, will have to report the
dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules.

If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—STATE AND CONSUMER SERVICES AGENCY                    GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor


DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES
EXECUTIVE OFFICE
915 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 590
SACRAMENTO, CA  95814




February 15, 1984


TO PROSPECTIVE BIDDERS:

Attached is the addenda for Request for Proposal DGS-84-001 entitled
Discounted Air Fares for State Travel. The addenda has been developed
from information received at the February 10, 1984 Bidders Conference
held in Sacramento.

If you have any questions, please contact Susan Hogg in Sacramento at
(916) 323-3071.


Jack D. Smith, Chief
Office of Program and
Compliance Evaluation

Attachment

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—STATE AND CONSUMER SERVICES AGENCY                    GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN, Governor

.DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES
EXECUTIVE OFFICE
915 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 590
SACRAMENTO, CA  95814




                                                                    MAY 13  9 25 AM 84




January 31, 1984


TO PROSPECTIVE BIDDERS:

You are invited to review and respond to the enclosed Request for
Proposal DGS-84-001 entitled Discounted Air Fares for State Travel.

If you have any questions, please contact Susan Hogg in Sacramento
at (916)323-3071.




[Signature]

Jack D. Smith, Chief
Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation




Attachment

REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL

FOR

DISCOUNTED AIR FARES
FOR STATE TRAVEL

RFP DGS-84-001




STATE OF CALIFORNIA




DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES
OFFICE OF PROGRAM AND COMPLIANCE EVALUATION
818 K STREET MALL, SUITE 100
SACRAMENTO, CA  95814

PAGE 2

TABLE OF CONTENTS


INTRODUCTION AND OVERVIEW OF
REQUIREMENTS                                    SECTION I

RULES GOVERNING COMPETITION              SECTION II

SERVICE TO BE PERFORMED                   SECTION III

COST                                      SECTION IV

PROPOSAL FORMAT                           SECTION V

EVALUATION                                SECTION VI

                                          ATTACHMENTS

ONE PERCENT SAMPLE OF 1979-80
TRAVEL CLAIMS FOR AIR TRANSPORTATION
FOR PRIMARY CITY-PAIRS                    A

ONE PERCENT SAMPLE OF 1979-80
TRAVEL CLAIMS FOR AIR TRANSPORTATION
FOR REMAINDER OF CITY-PAIRS               B

SCHEDULE OF ITEMS                         C

INSTRUCTIONS FOR SCREENING
FINAL PROPOSALS                           D

STANDARD FORM 2                           E

PAGE 3

SECTION I

INTRODUCTION AND OVERVIEW OF REQUIREMENTS

A. Purpose Of This Request for Proposal

The Department of General Services has been mandated by the Budget Act
of 1983-84, Chapter 326, Item 1760-001-666 to "...negotiate air fare
discounts..." for state travel. These discounted fares would be used
for authorized state business between the city-pairs shown in Attachment
A. A city-pair for purposes of this RFP is defined as travel between two
destinations. For example, the city-pair of Sacramento to Los Angeles
International requires flights from Sacramento to Los Angeles
International and from Los Angeles International to Sacramento.

The State currently spends $20 million annually for airline travel.
Based on a Department of General Services study conducted on travel
patterns for the 1979-80 fiscal year, there are approximately 222,300
one-way flights made each year for state business. Of this total, 176,800
flights costing approximately $11 million are necessary for employee
travel primarily between Northern and Southern California. The specific
airports affected are as follows: Sacramento; San Francisco; Oakland;
San Jose; Los Angeles International; Burbank; Orange County; Ontario; and
San Diego.

The purpose of this Request for Proposal(RFP) is to obtain discounted
air fares from air carriers. The specific city-pair identified in
Attachment A represent the primary travel needs of the State. The
additional routes identified in Attachment B represent the remainder of
State travel needs based on the sample of the 1979-80 travel patterns.

B. Official Department Contact

The Department Official contact person to whom to send proposals or
copies of protests to, or ask questions to, is:

Susan Hogg
Department of General Services
Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation
818 K Street Mall, Suite 100
Sacramento, CA 95814
Telephone: (916) 323-3071

PAGE 4

C.  Key Action Dates


     Listed below are the important actions and dates, and times in which
actions must be taken or completed.  If the State finds it necessary to
change any of these dates, it will be accomplished by addendum.


     Task                                    Date            Time

1.  Release of RFP                           1-31-84
2.  Bidders' Conference                      2-10-84         10:00 a.m.
3.  Confidential discussion                  1-31-84 to 2-17-84
    with individual contractors,
    if requested
4.  Last day to protest RFP                  2-17-84
5.  Submission of Final Proposal             3-16-84         2:00 p.m.
6.  Opening of proposals                     3-16-84         2:00 p.m.
7.  Public notification of
    intent to award                          4-5-84
8.  Last day to protest selection            4-12-84
9.  Contract award(s) and execution          4-13-84
10. Contractor(s) begin work                 7-1-84

PAGE 5

SECTION II

RULES GOVERNING COMPETITION

A. Proposing Requirements and Conditions

1. General

This Request for Proposal, the evaluation of responses, and the
award of any resultant contract(s) shall be made in conformance with
the rules of this section and applicable State law and policy. A
bidders Final Proposal will be taken as an irrevocable offer for 30
days following the scheduled date for contract award specified in
Section I.

2. Request for Proposal Documents

This RFP includes, in addition to an explanation of the State's
needs which must be met, instructions which prescribe the format and
content of the proposal to be submitted.

3. Examination of the Work

The bidders should carefully examine the entire RFP and addenda
thereto, and all related materials and data referenced in the RFP or
otherwise available, and should become fully aware of the nature and
location(s) of the services, the quantities of the services, and the
conditions to be encountered in providing the services.

4. Bidders' Conference

The Bidders' Conference will be held at the Department of General
Services, 915 Capitol Mall, Room 587, Sacramento, CA, 95814, on
February 10, 1984, at 10:00 a.m. The purpose of the Bidders'
Conference is to provide potential contractors an opportunity to
obtain clarification of the intent or content of this RFP.

5. Questions Regarding the RFP

Bidders requiring clarification of the intent or content of this
RFP may contact the Department Official listed in Section I,
paragraph B.

6. Confidential Discussions with Individual Bidders

PAGE 6

A potential contractor may request confidential discussions in
accordance with the dates specified in Section I. The purpose of
these discussions is to afford bidders the opportunity to meet with
state personnel to the discuss the RFP and the procurement process.

B. Steps of Proposal Development and Review

1. Final Proposal

The Final Proposal must be complete, including all cost
information and signatures on the cover letters and standard
agreement.

2. Submission of Proposals

The instructions contained herein apply to the Final Proposal.

a. Preparation

Proposals are to be prepared in such a way as to provide a
straightforward, concise delineation of capabilities to satisfy
the requirements of this RFP. EMPHASIS SHOULD BE CONCENTRATED ON
CONFORMANCE TO THE RFP INSTRUCTIONS, RESPONSIVENESS TO THE RFP
REQUIREMENTS, AND ON COMPLETENESS AND CLARITY OF CONTENT.

b. Bidders' Costs

Cost for developing proposals are the responsibility entirely
of the bidder and shall not be chargeable to the State.

c. Completion of Proposals

Proposals must be complete in all respects as required by the
RFP section on PROPOSAL FORMAT. A Final Proposal may be rejected
if it is conditional or incomplete, or if there are any
alterations of form or other irregularities of any kind.

d. Signature of Proposal

A cover letter, which shall be considered an integral part of
the Final Proposal, shall be signed by an individual(s) who
is(are) authorized to bind the bidder(s) contractually. The
signature(s) must indicate the title or position that the
individual(s) holds in the firm. The bidder(s) must not include
any limiting statements in the cover letter that would preclude
the Final Proposal from being held as an irrevocable offer for at
least 30 days after the scheduled award date.

An unsigned Final Proposal shall be rejected. A Final
Proposal may be signed by an agent(s) of the bidder(s) only if the
agent is an officer or a corporate contractor authorized to sign

PAGE 7

contracts on bidders behalf, a member of a partnership, or is
properly authorized by a power of attorney or equivalent
document. Such authorization must be submitted to the State
prior to the submission of proposals or with the proposals. The
name and title of the individuals(s) signing the proposal must be
typed immediately below the signature.

e. Delivery of Proposals

Mail or deliver proposals to the the Department of General
Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K
Street Mall, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan
Hogg. If mailed, use certified or registered mail with return
receipt requested.

Proposals received after 2:00 p.m., March 16, 1984, will not
be considered.

Proposals must be received in the number of copies stated in
the RFP section on PROPOSAL FORMAT, and not later than the dates
and times specified in SECTION I, paragraph C. One copy must be
clearly marked Proposal for Discounted Air Fares and 'Master
Copy'. They shall also be clearly marked 'CONFIDENTIAL' and
shall state the scheduled date and time for submission. Final
Proposals not received by the date and time specified in Section
I, or improperly sealed, will be rejected. Proposals submitted under
improperly marked covers may be rejected. If discrepancies are
found between two or more copies of the proposal, the proposal
may be rejected. However, if not so rejected, the 'Master Copy'
will provide the basis for resolving such discrepancies. BIDDERS
ARE TO SUBMIT THEIR PROPOSALS WITH THE BID PRICE AND ALL COST
INFORMATION IN A SEPARATE, SEALED ENVELOPE.

f. Withdrawal and Resubmission/Modification of Proposals

A bidder may withdraw the proposal at any time prior to final
bid due date by submitting a written notification of its
withdrawal signed by the bidder or agent authorized in accordance
with paragraph B 2 of this section (Signature of Proposal).
He/she may thereafter submit a new or modified proposal prior to
final bid due date. Modification offered in any other manner,
oral or written, will not be considered. A Final Proposal cannot
be changed or withdrawn after the time designated for receipt,
except as provided in paragraph 4 c of this section.

g. Joint Proposal

A joint proposal, that is, two or more bidders quoting jointly
on one proposal, may be submitted. Each participating bidder
must sign the joint proposal. If the contract is awarded to joint
contractors, it shall be one indivisible contract. Each joint
contract will be jointly and severally responsible for the
performance of the entire contract, and the joint contractors
must designate, in writing, one individual having authority to

PAGE 8

represent them in all matters relating to the contract. The
State assumes no responsibility or obligation for the division of
orders or purchases among the joint contractors.

3. Rejection of Proposals

The State may reject any or all proposals, and may or may not waive
any immaterial deviation or defect in a proposal. The State's waiver
of an immaterial deviation or defect shall in no way modify the RFP
documents or excuse the contractor from full compliance with the RFP
specifications if he/she is awarded the contract.


4. Evaluation and Selection Process

a. General

Proposals will be evaluated according to the procedures
contained in the RFP, Section VI, EVALUATION. The opening of
Final Proposals will be conducted at the Department of General
Services, 915 Capitol Mall, Room 587, Sacramento, CA, 95814, on
March 16, 1984, at 2:00 p.m.

b. Evaluation Questions

During the evaluation and selection process, the State may
desire the presence of a bidders representative for the purpose
of answering specific questions, orally and/or in writing.

c. Errors in the Final Proposal

If discrepancies between sections or other errors are found in
a Final Proposal, the State may reject the proposal; however, the
State may, at its sole option, retain the proposal and correct
any arithmetic errors in price on the basis that the proposal
narrative shall have priority over proposed contract clauses;
the proposed contract clauses shall have priority over the cost
sheets; and within each of these, the highest level of detail
will prevail.

If an item is described in the narrative and omitted from the
proposed contract and cost data provided in the Final Proposal
for evaluation purposes, it will be presumed to be provided by
the contractor at no cost.

IT IS ABSOLUTELY ESSENTIAL THAT THE BIDDER CAREFULLY REVIEW
THE COST ELEMENTS IN THE BIDDERS' FINAL PROPOSAL, SINCE THE
BIDDER WILL NOT HAVE THE OPTION TO CHANGE THEM AFTER THE TIME FOR
SUBMITTAL.

5. Award(s) of Contract(s)

PAGE 9

Award(s) of contract(s), if made, will be to the responsible
bidder selected in accordance with the RFP Section on EVALUATION, and
whose Final Proposal(s) complies with all the requirements of the RFP
documents and addenda thereto, except for such immaterial defects as
may be waived by the State. Award(s), if made, will be made within
thirty (30) days after the scheduled date for contract award
specified in Section I, paragraph C.

Written notification of the State's intent to award will be made
to all bidders submitting proposals. If a contractor, having
submitted a Final Proposal, can show that they, instead of the bidder
selected by the State, should be selected for contract/award
according to the rules of paragraph B 4 of this section, the bidder
will be allowed five (5) working days to submit a protest to the
intent to award, paragraph D 1 of this section.

The State reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to exclude
from any contracts awarded certain city-pairs.

C. Contractual Information

1. Contract Form

The State of California has a standard agreement (Standard Form 2)
which must be agreed upon by each bidder submitting a proposal in
response to this RFP. The standard agreement will incorporate the
attached terms and conditions(Attachment E), this RFP, the bidder's
response to this RFP, and a nondiscrimination clause(Attachment E).

Terms and conditions which do not comply in substance with all
material requirements of the RFP, which are contrary to the best
interests of the State, or which are in opposition to State policy,
will not be accepted and will be cause for rejection of the proposal.

2. Term of Contract

The State intends to retain the required services for one year.
The contract may be modified by mutual consent of both the State and
the contractor.

3. Termination or Cancellation

The State reserves the right to cancel any contract or contracts
awarded pursuant to this RFP upon 30 days notice.

D. Other Information

1. Protests

PAGE 10

If a bidder has submitted a proposal which the bidder believes to
be totally responsive to the requirements of the RFP and believes the
State has incorrectly selected another contractor for award(s), a
protest of the selection may be submitted as described below.

All protests must be made in writing, signed by an individual
authorized under paragraph B 2 d of this section (Signature of
Proposal), and contain a statement of the reason(s) for protest,
citing the law, rule, regulation, or procedure on which the protest
is based. The protestor must provide facts and evidence to support
the claim. Protests must be mailed or delivered to the Department of
General Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K
Street, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan Hogg.

All protests to the RFP or protests concerning the evaluation,
recommendation, or other aspects of the selection process must be
received as promptly as possible, but not later than the respective
times and dates specified in Section I, paragraph C, for such
protests. Certified or registered mail must be used unless delivered
in person, in which case the protestor should obtain a receipt of
delivery.


2. News Releases

News releases pertaining to the award resulting from the RFP shall
not be made without prior written approval of Department of General
Services, Office of Program and Compliance Evaluation, 818 K Street
Mall, Suite 100, Sacramento, CA, 95814, Attention: Susan Hogg.

3. Disposition of Proposals

All materials submitted in response to this RFP will become the
property of the State of California and will be returned, if
requested by a contractor, only at the State's option and the
contractor's expense. The Master Copy shall be retained for official
files and will become a public record after the date and time for
Final Proposal submission as specified in Section I, paragraph C (KEY
ACTION DATES). Confidential financial information submitted in
support of the requirement to show contractor responsibility will be
returned upon request.

4. Contacts for Information

Contractors may contact the Department Official listed in Section
I, paragraph B, for purposes of familiarization with the RFP
requirements. Visits shall be made by appointment only, during
normal business hours, and will be limited to the Department Official
or her designee(s).

sections.question_synthetic: Whether discounted air fares for personal travel by state officials negotiated as part of state contracts with commercial carriers constitute reportable income under FPPC rules.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to respond to the question pending the Attorney General's constitutional determination regarding the permissibility of the discounted air fare program, noting the question may become moot depending on that opinion.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains multiple copies of the same FPPC response letter (June 29, 1984) along with supporting memoranda from Department of General Services (May 3 and May 29, 1984) and RFP documentation. The FPPC response is a declination letter, not a substantive advice letter. OCR quality is good overall with some minor artifacts.

embedding.first_500_words: State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement (916) 322-5562 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 June 29, 1984 W. J. Anthony, Director Department of General Services State & Consumer Services Agency 915 Capitol Mall, Suite 590 Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Advice Our No. A-84-159 Dear Mr. Anthony: You have written requesting our advice on whether discounted air fares for personal travel by state officials is reportable income for those officials if the discount opportunity is negotiated by the state as part of its contract with commercial carriers for reduced-fare air travel for state employees and officials traveling on state business. We understand that you have submitted a separate request to the Attorney General on the issue of whether or not this is permissible under California Constitution, Art. 12, Section 19. Because the Attorney General's opinion could determine that such a program is prohibited by the Constitution, your question may be moot. Therefore, we decline to respond to it until the Attorney General has acted. If the Attorney General determines that such a program is permissible, we will then act promptly to give you advice. Sincerely, [signature] Robert E. Leidigh Counsel Legal Division REL:plh State of California State and Consumer Services Agency Memorandum MAY 31 8 33 AM '84 Date : May 29, 1984 File No. : To : Dan Stanford, Chairperson Subject : Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 K Street -- D-23 From : Department of General Services On May 3, 1984, I asked for your advice on whether an officer and employee of the State, taking advantage of the discounted rates for personal trips, under the contracts being consummated with PSA and Delta Airlines, will have to report the dollar value of the discounted rate under your rules. We have now determined that the extension of the discounted rates to management personnel for personal trips will not be included in the con- tract for this year. However, we would appreciate your advice on the question asked in our memorandum, inasmuch as we will have that problem before us as we enter into contract negotiations next year. If you wish more information on this subject, you may wish to have your staff contact: Jack Smith, Chief, Office of Program and Compliance Evalua- tion (323-3066), or Emil J. Relat, Chief Counsel, Office of Legal Services (322-5944). [signature] W. J. Under the contract, the airlines
embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter (A-84-159) from June 29, 1984, declining to provide advice on whether discounted personal air fares for state officials constitute reportable income, pending the Attorney General's determination on whether such a program is constitutionally permissible under California Constitution Article 12, Section 19.
